cards.back: A fundamental (indivisible) particle that feels the strong force. A-level physics needs only the **up** ($+\frac{2}{3}e$) and **down** ($-\frac{1}{3}e$) quarks.
A fundamental particle that does **not** feel the strong force, e.g. the **electron** ($-1e$) and the **electron neutrino** (chargeless, almost massless).
Any particle made of quarks (held together by the strong force). Protons and neutrons are hadrons.
A hadron made of **three** quarks, e.g. the proton (uud) and the neutron (udd).

cards.front: What is the quark composition of a **proton**?
What is the quark composition of a **neutron**?
Which particle is the carrier (exchange boson) of the **electromagnetic** force?
Which particle is the carrier of the **strong** force between quarks?
What must be conserved in every particle interaction or decay (besides energy and momentum)?
